---
title: "Lecture 22: HTTP GET & POST Requests"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "March 17, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 22]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[HTTP GET & POST Requests]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 8 | Unit III: JSP & Servlets Introduction<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand HTTP request methods
- Differentiate between GET and POST requests
- Implement `doGet()` and `doPost()` methods
- Handle HTML form submissions
- Extract request parameters
- Send appropriate HTTP responses
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="HTTP Methods"}

[HTTP Request Methods]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Common HTTP Methods:**

- **GET**: Retrieve data from server
- **POST**: Send data to server
- **PUT**: Update existing resource
- **DELETE**: Remove resource
- **HEAD**: Get headers only
- **OPTIONS**: Get supported methods

In servlets, we primarily use **GET** and **POST**.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Request Response Cycle"}

[HTTP Request-Response Cycle]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant Browser
    participant WebServer
    participant Servlet
    participant Database
    
    Browser->>WebServer: HTTP Request (GET/POST)
    WebServer->>Servlet: Forward Request
    Servlet->>Database: Query Data
    Database-->>Servlet: Return Data
    Servlet-->>WebServer: HTTP Response
    WebServer-->>Browser: Display Response
```

---

## {data-menu-title="GET Method"}

[GET Method]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Purpose**: Request data from server

**Characteristics:**

- Data sent in URL query string
- Limited data size (~2KB)
- Visible in browser address bar
- Can be bookmarked
- Can be cached
- **Not secure** for sensitive data
- Idempotent (same result on repeated calls)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="GET Example"}

[GET Request Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

**URL with Query Parameters:**
```
http://localhost:8080/app/search?query=java&category=books
```

**HTML Form (GET):**
```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
<form action="search" method="GET">
  <input type="text" name="query" placeholder="Search term">
  <input type="text" name="category" value="books">
  <button type="submit">Search</button>
</form>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="doGet Method"}

[Handling GET Requests: doGet()]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-6|9-10|13-18"}
@WebServlet("/search")
public class SearchServlet extends HttpServlet {
  
  protected void doGet(HttpServletRequest request,
                      HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    
    // Get query parameters from URL
    String query = request.getParameter("query");
    String category = request.getParameter("category");
    
    // Generate response
    response.setContentType("text/html");
    PrintWriter out = response.getWriter();
    out.println("<h1>Search Results</h1>");
    out.println("<p>Query: " + query + "</p>");
    out.println("<p>Category: " + category + "</p>");
    out.close();
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="GET Use Cases"}

[When to Use GET]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- **Search operations**: Searching products, articles
- **Filtering data**: Sort, filter options
- **Pagination**: Page numbers in URL
- **Sharing links**: Bookmarkable URLs
- **Read-only operations**: No data modification
- **Public data**: Non-sensitive information
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-m}
**Example URLs:**
```
/products?category=electronics&sort=price
/articles?page=2&limit=10
/weather?city=Delhi&units=metric
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="POST Method"}

[POST Method]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Purpose**: Submit data to server

**Characteristics:**

- Data sent in request body (hidden)
- Unlimited data size
- Not visible in URL
- Cannot be bookmarked
- Not cached
- **More secure** than GET
- Non-idempotent (may have different results)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="POST Example"}

[POST Request Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

**HTML Form (POST):**
```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-3|4-5|6"}
<form action="register" method="POST">
  <input type="text" name="username" required>
  <input type="email" name="email" required>
  <input type="password" name="password" required>
  <input type="date" name="dob">
  <button type="submit">Register</button>
</form>
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Form data sent in request body, not visible in URL.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="doPost Method"}

[Handling POST Requests: doPost()]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-9|12-13|16-21"}
@WebServlet("/register")
public class RegisterServlet extends HttpServlet {
  
  protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request,
                       HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    
    // Get form parameters from request body
    String username = request.getParameter("username");
    String email = request.getParameter("email");
    String password = request.getParameter("password");
    String dob = request.getParameter("dob");
    
    // Process registration (save to database)
    boolean success = saveUser(username, email, password, dob);
    
    // Send response
    response.setContentType("text/html");
    PrintWriter out = response.getWriter();
    if (success) {
      out.println("<h1>Registration Successful!</h1>");
      out.println("<p>Welcome, " + username + "</p>");
    } else {
      out.println("<h1>Registration Failed</h1>");
    }
    out.close();
  }
  
  private boolean saveUser(String username, String email, 
                          String password, String dob) {
    // Database logic here
    return true;
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="POST Use Cases"}

[When to Use POST]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- **User registration**: Creating new accounts
- **Login forms**: Authenticating users
- **Data submission**: Creating/updating records
- **File uploads**: Sending files to server
- **Sensitive data**: Passwords, credit cards
- **Large data**: No size limitations
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-m}
**Examples:**
- User registration and login
- Contact form submission
- Blog post creation
- Order placement
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="GET vs POST"}

[GET vs POST Comparison]{.slide-title}

<hr>

| Feature | GET | POST |
|---------|-----|------|
| **Data Location** | URL query string | Request body |
| **Visibility** | Visible in URL | Hidden |
| **Data Size** | Limited (~2KB) | Unlimited |
| **Security** | Less secure | More secure |
| **Bookmarking** | Can bookmark | Cannot bookmark |
| **Caching** | Can be cached | Not cached |
| **Browser History** | Saved | Not saved |
| **Use Case** | Retrieve data | Submit data |
| **Idempotent** | Yes | No |

---

## {data-menu-title="Request Parameters"}

[Extracting Request Parameters]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|2|5|8|11-14"}
// Single parameter value
String name = request.getParameter("name");

// Multiple values for same parameter (checkboxes)
String[] hobbies = request.getParameterValues("hobby");

// All parameter names
Enumeration<String> paramNames = request.getParameterNames();

// All parameters as Map
Map<String, String[]> paramMap = request.getParameterMap();
for (Map.Entry<String, String[]> entry : paramMap.entrySet()) {
  System.out.println(entry.getKey() + ": " + 
                     Arrays.toString(entry.getValue()));
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Checkbox Example"}

[Handling Multiple Values (Checkboxes)]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

**HTML Form:**
```{.html code-line-numbers="|2-5"}
<form action="preferences" method="POST">
  <input type="checkbox" name="hobby" value="coding"> Coding
  <input type="checkbox" name="hobby" value="music"> Music
  <input type="checkbox" name="hobby" value="sports"> Sports
  <button type="submit">Submit</button>
</form>
```

**Servlet:**
```{.java code-line-numbers="|2|3-5"}
protected void doPost(...) {
  String[] hobbies = request.getParameterValues("hobby");
  if (hobbies != null) {
    for (String hobby : hobbies) {
      System.out.println("Hobby: " + hobby);
    }
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Request Info"}

[Request Information Methods]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|2|5|8|11|14|17"}
// HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)
String method = request.getMethod();

// Request URI
String uri = request.getRequestURI();  // /app/login

// Query string (for GET)
String query = request.getQueryString();  // user=john&id=5

// Request URL
StringBuffer url = request.getRequestURL();

// Context path
String contextPath = request.getContextPath();  // /app

// Servlet path
String servletPath = request.getServletPath();  // /login
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Response Methods"}

[Response Methods]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|2|5|8|11"}
// Set content type
response.setContentType("text/html; charset=UTF-8");

// Get writer for text output
PrintWriter out = response.getWriter();

// Set HTTP status code
response.setStatus(HttpServletResponse.SC_OK);  // 200

// Redirect to another page
response.sendRedirect("success.html");
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Content Types"}

[Common Content Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
```java
// HTML page
response.setContentType("text/html");

// Plain text
response.setContentType("text/plain");

// JSON data
response.setContentType("application/json");

// XML data
response.setContentType("application/xml");

// PDF file
response.setContentType("application/pdf");
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Complete Login"}

[Complete Login Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1|3-5|8-9|12-18|20-22"}
@WebServlet("/login")
public class LoginServlet extends HttpServlet {
  
  protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request,
                       HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    
    String username = request.getParameter("username");
    String password = request.getParameter("password");
    
    // Validate credentials
    if (authenticate(username, password)) {
      // Success - redirect to home
      HttpSession session = request.getSession();
      session.setAttribute("user", username);
      response.sendRedirect("home.jsp");
    } else {
      // Failure - show error
      response.setContentType("text/html");
      PrintWriter out = response.getWriter();
      out.println("<h1>Login Failed!</h1>");
      out.println("<a href='login.html'>Try Again</a>");
    }
  }
  
  private boolean authenticate(String user, String pass) {
    // Database authentication logic
    return "admin".equals(user) && "password".equals(pass);
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Redirect vs Forward"}

[Redirect vs Forward]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**sendRedirect()** - Client-side redirect

```java
response.sendRedirect("success.jsp");
```
- New HTTP request
- URL changes in browser
- Can redirect to external sites

**RequestDispatcher.forward()** - Server-side forward

```java
RequestDispatcher rd = request.getRequestDispatcher("result.jsp");
rd.forward(request, response);
```
- Same HTTP request
- URL unchanged in browser
- Internal forwarding only
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Error Handling"}

[Error Handling]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-8|10-14"}
protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request,
                     HttpServletResponse response)
  throws ServletException, IOException {
  
  try {
    String age = request.getParameter("age");
    int ageValue = Integer.parseInt(age);
    // Process age
  } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
    response.setStatus(HttpServletResponse.SC_BAD_REQUEST);
    PrintWriter out = response.getWriter();
    out.println("Invalid age format!");
  } catch (Exception e) {
    response.sendError(500, "Internal Server Error");
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Status Codes"}

[HTTP Status Codes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.body-text-m}
**Success (2xx)**

- `200 OK` - Request successful
- `201 Created` - Resource created

**Redirection (3xx)**

- `301 Moved Permanently`
- `302 Found (Temporary redirect)`
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.body-text-m}
**Client Error (4xx)**

- `400 Bad Request`
- `404 Not Found`
- `403 Forbidden`

**Server Error (5xx)**

- `500 Internal Server Error`
- `503 Service Unavailable`
:::
:::

::::

---

## {data-menu-title="Form to Servlet"}

[Complete Form to Servlet Flow]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

**registration.html:**
```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-4|5"}
<form action="register" method="POST">
  <input type="text" name="username" required>
  <input type="email" name="email" required>
  <input type="password" name="password" required>
  <button type="submit">Register</button>
</form>
```

**RegisterServlet.java:**
```{.java code-line-numbers="|2|3-5|6-7"}
protected void doPost(...) {
  // Extract form data
  String username = request.getParameter("username");
  String email = request.getParameter("email");
  String password = request.getParameter("password");
  // Process and respond
  response.sendRedirect("welcome.jsp");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Both Methods"}

[Handling Both GET and POST]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1-3|6-10|13-16"}
@WebServlet("/user")
public class UserServlet extends HttpServlet {
  
  // Show user form (GET)
  protected void doGet(HttpServletRequest request,
                      HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    response.setContentType("text/html");
    // Display form
  }
  
  // Process form submission (POST)
  protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request,
                       HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    // Process form data
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="POST Redirect GET"}

[POST-Redirect-GET Pattern]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant Browser
    participant Servlet
    
    Browser->>Servlet: POST /register (form data)
    Servlet->>Servlet: Process & Save Data
    Servlet-->>Browser: 302 Redirect to /success
    Browser->>Servlet: GET /success
    Servlet-->>Browser: 200 Success Page
```

::: {.body-text-m}
**Benefits:**
- Prevents duplicate submissions on page refresh
- Cleaner browser history
- Better user experience
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Calculator Example"}

[Calculator Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

**HTML Form:**
```{.html code-line-numbers="|2-5|6"}
<form action="calculate" method="POST">
  <input type="number" name="num1" required>
  <select name="operation">
    <option value="add">+</option>
    <option value="subtract">-</option>
  </select>
  <input type="number" name="num2" required>
  <button type="submit">Calculate</button>
</form>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Calculator Servlet"}

[Calculator Servlet]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|5-7|10-21"}
@WebServlet("/calculate")
public class CalculatorServlet extends HttpServlet {
  
  protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request,
                       HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
    
    int num1 = Integer.parseInt(request.getParameter("num1"));
    int num2 = Integer.parseInt(request.getParameter("num2"));
    String operation = request.getParameter("operation");
    
    int result = 0;
    switch(operation) {
      case "add": result = num1 + num2; break;
      case "subtract": result = num1 - num2; break;
      case "multiply": result = num1 * num2; break;
      case "divide": result = num1 / num2; break;
    }
    
    response.setContentType("text/html");
    response.getWriter().println("<h1>Result: " + result + "</h1>");
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Security Best Practices"}

[Security Best Practices]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use POST for sensitive data (passwords, personal info)
- Validate and sanitize all input
- Use HTTPS for production
- Protect against SQL injection
- Implement CSRF tokens for forms
- Encode output to prevent XSS attacks
- Use parameterized queries
- Never trust client-side validation alone
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Input Validation"}

[Input Validation Example]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|2-4|7-9|12-14"}
// Validate required parameters
if (username == null || username.trim().isEmpty()) {
  response.sendError(400, "Username is required");
  return;
}

// Validate format (email)
if (!email.matches("^[A-Za-z0-9+_.-]+@(.+)$")) {
  response.sendError(400, "Invalid email format");
  return;
}

// Validate length
if (password.length() < 8) {
  response.sendError(400, "Password must be at least 8 characters");
  return;
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Live Demo"}

[Live Classroom Demo: GET & POST]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Use this interactive page during class to demonstrate:

- GET and POST request differences
- Form data extraction
- Query parameter handling
- Request/response visualization
- Calculator with both methods
:::

::: {.body-text-l .center-text .topbr}
[Open Demo: GET & POST Requests](../html/demos/lecture-22-demo.html){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text .topbr}
Alternative site path: `/html/demos/lecture-22-demo.html`
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text}
[Open app.js Source](../html/demos/lecture-22/app.js){target="_blank"}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes to Avoid]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Using GET for sensitive data
- Not validating input parameters
- Forgetting to set content type
- Not handling null parameters
- Hardcoding URLs in redirects
- Ignoring character encoding
- Not closing PrintWriter
- Mixing HTML and Java code excessively
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Summary"}

[Summary]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- GET retrieves data, POST submits data
- GET: data in URL, POST: data in body
- Use `doGet()` for GET requests
- Use `doPost()` for POST requests
- Extract parameters using `request.getParameter()`
- Set content type before writing response
- Validate all user input
- Use POST for sensitive/large data
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice"}

[Practice Exercise]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Create a Student Registration System:

1. HTML form with fields: name, email, program, year
2. Servlet to handle POST submission
3. Validate all fields
4. Display confirmation page with entered data
5. Add a "View All Students" GET endpoint

**Bonus**: Implement search functionality using GET with query parameters
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: HTTP Methods](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Methods)
- [Jakarta Servlet Specification](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/)
- [HTTP Status Codes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status)
- [Course content: Week 8](/content/week-08.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 23 - Session Tracking & Cookies
</div>
</div>
